mission.title: Install and Configure Stacker on Kubernetes
mission.description: Stacker is a tool for building OCI images natively using a declarative YAML format. Installing Stacker allows you to create container images in a vendor-neutral manner, leveraging its rootless and hermetically sealed build capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Stacker Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Stacker Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add stacker https://stackerbuild.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Stacker using Helm
steps.description:
Install Stacker in a new namespace called 'stacker'. This command will deploy Stacker with the specified version.
```bash
helm install stacker stacker/stacker --version 1.1.6 --namespace stacker --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify Stacker installation
steps.description:
Check if the Stacker pods are running successfully in the 'stacker' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace stacker
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Stacker
steps.description:
Update the Stacker deployment to set resource limits for better performance management.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/stacker --namespace stacker --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Port forward to access Stacker service
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Stacker service locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/stacker --namespace stacker 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Stacker will show the Stacker pods running in the 'stacker' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. You can also verify that resource limits are applied to the Stacker deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add stacker https://stackerbuild.io/charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Stacker.
